snippets.title: General penalty
snippets.summary: Used for offences with no specific penalty. First offence: ₹500. Subsequent: ₹1,500.

snippets.title: Driving without a valid licence
snippets.summary: Imprisonment up to 3 months and/or fine up to ₹5,000. Compoundable in most states.

snippets.title: Over-speeding
snippets.summary: LMV: ₹1,000–₹2,000. Medium/Heavy: ₹2,000–₹4,000. Licence may be impounded for repeat offences.

snippets.title: Dangerous driving / signal jumping / mobile use
snippets.summary: First offence: ₹1,000–₹5,000 and/or 6 months prison. Repeat: up to ₹10,000 and 2 years prison.

snippets.title: Driving under influence
snippets.summary: ₹10,000 and/or 6 months imprisonment for first offence. Non-compoundable. Repeat within 3 years: ₹15,000 and/or 2 years.

snippets.title: Racing & speed trials on public roads
snippets.summary: Non-compoundable. ₹5,000 and/or 3 months imprisonment for first offence; ₹10,000 and/or 1 year for repeat.

snippets.title: Driving an unsafe / polluting vehicle
snippets.summary: ₹10,000 per offence; licence may be disqualified for 3 months.

snippets.title: Overloading of goods
snippets.summary: ₹20,000 plus ₹2,000 per excess tonne. The vehicle may be detained until the load is reduced.

snippets.title: Seat-belt violation
snippets.summary: ₹1,000 per offence. Applies to driver and all named seated occupants.

snippets.title: Two-wheeler with more than two persons
Riding without protective headgear
snippets.summary: ₹1,000 plus 3-month licence disqualification. Helmet must conform to BIS standard IS 4151.

snippets.title: Driving without valid insurance
snippets.summary: First offence: ₹2,000 and/or 3 months prison. Repeat: ₹4,000.

snippets.title: Power to detain vehicles
snippets.summary: An authorised officer may detain a vehicle used without a valid permit, registration, or licence.

rights: You have the right to ask for the officer's name, rank and badge number.
You may request a copy of the challan with the section cited and the officer's signature.
Spot fines above the officer's authority cap must be referred to a Magistrate; demand a court challan in writing.
Compoundable offences can be paid online, in person, or contested in court within the statutory deadline (typically 60 days).
You may inspect the speed-radar / breath-analyser calibration certificate; uncalibrated equipment is not admissible evidence.
